title: ophioglossum pendulum subsp. falcatum
intro: Ophioglossum pendulum subsp. falcatum, commonly known as the "hanging adder's tongue," is a type of fern that belongs to the family Ophioglossaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its ability to treat digestive issues and respiratory problems due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Ophioglossum pendulum subsp. falcatum include reducing inflammation, relieving coughs and colds, and aiding in the treatment of gastrointestinal disorders. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
